![CPS-VO logo](/themes/custom/cpsvo_refactor/images/cpsvo_logo.webp)

# Cyber-Physical Systems Virtual Organization

Fostering collaboration among CPS professionals in academia, government, and industry

## Hamburger Menu

## Front Page

![](/sites/cps-vo.org/files/2023-08/tools.webp)
![](/sites/cps-vo.org/files/2023-08/nav_research_projects.webp)
![](/sites/cps-vo.org/files/2022-05/about_meetings.png)
![](/sites/cps-vo.org/files/2023-08/collaborators.webp)

These tools and design studios facilitate modeling, verification, simulation, analysis, and other categories of tools that are of prime interest to the community. [Explore tools](/tools).

A forum for stakeholders in academia, industry, and federal agencies to review new developments in CPS foundations, to identify emerging applications, and to discuss technology gaps and barriers. View [PI Meeting archives](/meetings).

The [NSF CPS program](https://new.nsf.gov/funding/opportunities/cyber-physical-systems-cps) supports research on engineered systems with a seamless integration of cyber and physical components, such as computation, control, networking, learning, autonomy, security, privacy and verification, for a range of application domains.

The objective of the National Science Foundation established CPS Virtual Organization (CPS-VO) is to actively support the multidisciplinary CPS community and to foster interaction and exchanges among CPS PIs and their teams.

*The CPS-VO is an active resource that provides access to tools and methods emerging from the CPS research community as well as a collaboration platform and repository of information. The integrated suite of models, integration platforms, and intellectual frameworks are developed and contributed by the research community lead to a new era of low-cost, distributed and open design infrastructure.*

Full-length article

An integrated outlook of Cyber–Physical Systems for Industry 4.0: Topical practices, architecture, and applications
========================================================================================================================================

Author links open overlay panel Mohd Javaid a, Abid Haleem a, Ravi Pratap Singh b, Rajiv Suman c

Show more

Add to Mendeley

Share

Open access

Highlights
----------

*   •Industry 4.0 requires a strong understanding of Cyber–Physical systems (CPS). 
*   •Industry 4.0 enable manufacturing increase collaboration through CPS. 
*   •This paper brief about CPS and its need for Industry 4.0. 
*   •Embedded Processes and Smart 5C Architecture of CPS for Industry 4.0 are elaborated. 
*   •Finally, paper identifies and discusses significant applications of CPS for Industry 4.0. 

Abstract
--------

[Industry 4.0](https://www.sciencedirect.com/topics/computer-science/industry-4-0 "Learn more about Industry 4.0 from ScienceDirect's AI-generated Topic Pages") requires a strong understanding of Cyber–Physical Systems (CPS). An Industry 4.0-enabled [manufacturing environment](https://www.sciencedirect.com/topics/engineering/design-and-environment "Learn more about manufacturing environment from ScienceDirect's AI-generated Topic Pages") that offers real-time data gathering, transparency and analysis across all parts of a manufacturing process is known as cyber–physical systems, also known as cyber manufacturing. [Data analytics](https://www.sciencedirect.com/topics/computer-science/data-analytics "Learn more about Data analytics from ScienceDirect's AI-generated Topic Pages") enables executives to make data-driven choices and boost productivity, while automation speeds up manufacturing and decreases [machine downtime](https://www.sciencedirect.com/topics/engineering/machine-downtime "Learn more about machine downtime from ScienceDirect's AI-generated Topic Pages"). The main objective of deploying Industry 4.0 solutions is to enable manufacturing organisations to increase collaboration by making the correct [information accessible](https://www.sciencedirect.com/topics/computer-science/accessible-information "Learn more about information accessible from ScienceDirect's AI-generated Topic Pages") to the right people in real time. The aim of encouraging optimal decision-making at the appropriate moment is to improve efficiency and production further. The critical terms in Industry 4.0, such as the CPS, [Internet of Things](https://www.sciencedirect.com/topics/physics-and-astronomy/internet-of-things "Learn more about Internet of Things from ScienceDirect's AI-generated Topic Pages") (IoT), and [Digital Twin](https://www.sciencedirect.com/topics/computer-science/digital-twin "Learn more about Digital Twin from ScienceDirect's AI-generated Topic Pages"), are widely used interchangeably in conversations about smart manufacturing. These are essential to Industry 4.0 and smart manufacturing because they give users access to real-time operating data of the equipment they represent. [Cyber components](https://www.sciencedirect.com/topics/computer-science/cyber-component "Learn more about Cyber components from ScienceDirect's AI-generated Topic Pages") and physical components make up the two elements of CPS. The main aim of this paper is to brief CPS and its need for Industry 4.0. Embedded Processes and Smart 5C diagrammatically elaborate architecture of CPS for Industry 4.0. Finally, the paper identifies and discusses significant applications of CPS in Industry 4.0. For this paper, we identified and then studied relevant literature on CPS for establishing an Industry 4.0 environment. CPS is integrated into several items, including vehicles and other equipment, to carry out particular functions. CPS can be utilised in any industry, including engineering, manufacturing, transportation, and even health care, because they are all easy to use. CPS network [collaborative systems](https://www.sciencedirect.com/topics/computer-science/collaborative-systems "Learn more about collaborative systems from ScienceDirect's AI-generated Topic Pages") are built for communication. Furthermore, cutting-edge network technologies like cloud solutions are used by CPS.

*   [Previous article in issue](https://www.sciencedirect.com/science/article/pii/S2949736122000021)
*   [Next article in issue](https://www.sciencedirect.com/science/article/pii/S2949736122000033)

Cyber–Physical Systems (CPS)

Industry 4.0

Manufacturing

1. Introduction
---------------

Cyber–Physical Systems (CPS) are considered the next step in an evolution of continual refinement and function integration within [Industry 4.0](https://www.sciencedirect.com/topics/computer-science/industry-4-0 "Learn more about Industry 4.0 from ScienceDirect's AI-generated Topic Pages"). Semi-autonomous and autonomous decisions are vital components of Industry 4.0, with [IoT](https://www.sciencedirect.com/topics/physics-and-astronomy/internet-of-things "Learn more about IoT from ScienceDirect's AI-generated Topic Pages"), [Artificial Intelligence](https://www.sciencedirect.com/topics/computer-science/artificial-intelligence "Learn more about Artificial Intelligence from ScienceDirect's AI-generated Topic Pages") (AI), new [integrated systems](https://www.sciencedirect.com/topics/computer-science/integrated-system "Learn more about integrated systems from ScienceDirect's AI-generated Topic Pages"), advanced analytics, and other technologies all playing a role[[1]](https://www.sciencedirect.com/science/article/pii/S294973612200001X#b1), [[2]](https://www.sciencedirect.com/science/article/pii/S294973612200001X#b2), [[3]](https://www.sciencedirect.com/science/article/pii/S294973612200001X#b3). All types of industrial businesses, including discrete and process manufacturing, as well as oil and gas, mining, and other industrial segments, can use the ideas and technologies of Industry 4.0. [IoT](https://www.sciencedirect.com/topics/computer-science/internet-of-things "Learn more about IoT from ScienceDirect's AI-generated Topic Pages") is frequently utilised by smart manufacturing. In order to connect with other web-enabled devices, manufacturing equipment is equipped with sensors that have an IP address. This mechanisation and connection allow the collecting, analysing, and sharing significant volumes of valuable data[[4]](https://www.sciencedirect.com/science/article/pii/S294973612200001X#b4), [[5]](https://www.sciencedirect.com/science/article/pii/S294973612200001X#b5).

The manufacture of personalised items can be done more efficiently and flexibly, which are essential benefits of this revolution. Flexibility could generate one-of-a-kind, user-defined, or one-off items and allow producers to make alterations at the last minute during various production phases. Decision-making and openness in the production process are both enhanced. Efficiency in manufacturing and production has also significantly increased. For firms aiming to take advantage of Industry 4.0, creating a hybrid multi-cloud IT infrastructure is a crucial component of digital transformation. Hybrid multi-cloud is the management of an organisation’s [cloud computing](https://www.sciencedirect.com/topics/computer-science/cloud-computing "Learn more about cloud computing from ScienceDirect's AI-generated Topic Pages") workloads. They may optimise their workloads across all their clouds since some environments are more appropriate or economical for particular workloads. Manufacturers may move their current workloads from on-premises to the ideal cloud environment available if they want to undergo digital transformation and work in a safe and open environment[[6]](https://www.sciencedirect.com/science/article/pii/S294973612200001X#b6), [[7]](https://www.sciencedirect.com/science/article/pii/S294973612200001X#b7), [[8]](https://www.sciencedirect.com/science/article/pii/S294973612200001X#b8).

For real-time production activities, some data analysis must be done at the “edge” or where the data is created. It reduces the time between when data is generated and when a response is required. For instance, immediate action may be required if the equipment’s safety or quality is compromised. Depending on network connectivity, the time needed to transport data from the business cloud to the manufacturing floor may be considerable[[9]](https://www.sciencedirect.com/science/article/pii/S294973612200001X#b9), [[10]](https://www.sciencedirect.com/science/article/pii/S294973612200001X#b10), [[11]](https://www.sciencedirect.com/science/article/pii/S294973612200001X#b11). Additionally, [edge computing](https://www.sciencedirect.com/topics/computer-science/edge-computing "Learn more about edge computing from ScienceDirect's AI-generated Topic Pages") keeps data near its origin, reducing security threats. The [Industrial Internet of Things](https://www.sciencedirect.com/topics/computer-science/industrial-internet-of-things "Learn more about Industrial Internet of Things from ScienceDirect's AI-generated Topic Pages") (IIoT) utilises sophisticated control technology, Machine Learning (ML), and [big data](https://www.sciencedirect.com/topics/engineering/big-data "Learn more about big data from ScienceDirect's AI-generated Topic Pages") to allow smart machines, and eventually smart factories and warehouses, by [connecting sensor](https://www.sciencedirect.com/topics/computer-science/connecting-sensor "Learn more about connecting sensor from ScienceDirect's AI-generated Topic Pages") data, machine communications, and [automation systems](https://www.sciencedirect.com/topics/computer-science/automation-system "Learn more about automation systems from ScienceDirect's AI-generated Topic Pages") that have become prevalent in industrial settings. This technology is already being utilised through real-time condition monitoring to enhance how industrial systems are maintained and serviced[[12]](https://www.sciencedirect.com/science/article/pii/S294973612200001X#b12), [[13]](https://www.sciencedirect.com/science/article/pii/S294973612200001X#b13), [[14]](https://www.sciencedirect.com/science/article/pii/S294973612200001X#b14).

Industry 4.0 technologies necessitate increased connectivity, protecting critical industrial systems and manufacturing lines from cyber-attacks. Cybersecurity is used by businesses to protect their networks, systems, and data from cyber threats. This can provide users with real-time data to help them make decisions and improve their work output using augmented-reality glasses, eye-pieces, mobile devices, and other products[[15]](https://www.sciencedirect.com/science/article/pii/S294973612200001X#b15), [[16]](https://www.sciencedirect.com/science/article/pii/S294973612200001X#b16). Augmented reality (AR) technology allows users to work and make decisions independently by providing access to the correct information at the right time. While vast and deep networks of [IoT](https://www.sciencedirect.com/topics/engineering/internet-of-things "Learn more about IoT from ScienceDirect's AI-generated Topic Pages") devices are being built, the aspect of security compatibility among [IoT devices](https://www.sciencedirect.com/topics/computer-science/internet-of-things-device "Learn more about IoT devices from ScienceDirect's AI-generated Topic Pages") is being overlooked. Hundreds to thousands of IoT devices can be found on a single network in industries such as maritime, mining, healthcare, and retail. These devices are made by various manufacturers and have varying firmware and security requirements[[17]](https://www.sciencedirect.com/science/article/pii/S294973612200001X#b17), [[18]](https://www.sciencedirect.com/science/article/pii/S294973612200001X#b18). The main aim of this paper is to study the [potential applications](https://www.sciencedirect.com/topics/computer-science/potential-application "Learn more about potential applications from ScienceDirect's AI-generated Topic Pages") of CPS in Industry 4.0

2. Cyber–Physical Systems
-------------------------

A new generation of physical process integrations with computer and networking processes are known as ”Cyber–Physical Systems”. It is the blending of the physical world with cyberspace. Cyberspace is used to describe the widely used, networked, and computerised digital technology that is characterised by its infrastructure for communication and computation. CPS is used in various industries, including manufacturing, energy, infrastructure, consumer goods, military, robots, [smart buildings](https://www.sciencedirect.com/topics/engineering/intelligent-buildings "Learn more about smart buildings from ScienceDirect's AI-generated Topic Pages"), communications, healthcare, and transportation[[19]](https://www.sciencedirect.com/science/article/pii/S294973612200001X#b19), [[20]](https://www.sciencedirect.com/science/article/pii/S294973612200001X#b20), [[21]](https://www.sciencedirect.com/science/article/pii/S294973612200001X#b21). Automation and self-optimisation may both be utilised to make things better. CPS combines computing, storage, and communication capabilities with monitoring and control of [physical elements](https://www.sciencedirect.com/topics/computer-science/physical-element "Learn more about physical elements from ScienceDirect's AI-generated Topic Pages"). That is, give [physical objects](https://www.sciencedirect.com/topics/computer-science/physical-object "Learn more about physical objects from ScienceDirect's AI-generated Topic Pages") “intelligence” to communicate with one another. Because they are interconnected, they use global digital networks to monitor, control, and use the information available in the virtual world and learn, cooperate, and evolve. This technology has a wide range of applications and can be used in almost any industry[[22]](https://www.sciencedirect.com/science/article/pii/S294973612200001X#b22), [[23]](https://www.sciencedirect.com/science/article/pii/S294973612200001X#b23).

In the context of [Industry 4.0](https://www.sciencedirect.com/topics/computer-science/industry-4-0 "Learn more about Industry 4.0 from ScienceDirect's AI-generated Topic Pages"), a CPS is a fusion of physical parts, transducers, and information technology systems. CPS is sometimes defined as incorporating people, such as machine operators. A CPS is, in other words, a physical world [system connected](https://www.sciencedirect.com/topics/engineering/connected-system "Learn more about system connected from ScienceDirect's AI-generated Topic Pages") to the cyber world. A CPS can be an open-loop or closed-loop system, which implies that it can perceive the physical system’s real-world characteristics and regulate it or only make those data accessible for study. An Industry 4.0-enabled manufacturing enhanced cyber manufacturing, which offers real-time data gathering, analysis, and transparency across all areas of a manufacturing process. Businesses benefit from enhanced visibility, control, and data visibility across their supply chain using industry 4.0 technologies. By utilising [supply chain management](https://www.sciencedirect.com/topics/earth-and-planetary-sciences/supply-chain-management "Learn more about supply chain management from ScienceDirect's AI-generated Topic Pages") capabilities to get goods and services to market more quickly, affordably, and of higher quality, businesses may gain an edge over lesser effective rivals[[24]](https://www.sciencedirect.com/science/article/pii/S294973612200001X#b24), [[25]](https://www.sciencedirect.com/science/article/pii/S294973612200001X#b25), [[26]](https://www.sciencedirect.com/science/article/pii/S294973612200001X#b26).

3. Industry 4.0
---------------

The fourth industrial revolution, known as ”Industry 4.0”, involves the [digitalisation](https://www.sciencedirect.com/topics/computer-science/digitalization "Learn more about digitalisation from ScienceDirect's AI-generated Topic Pages") and automation of industry. A significant change in how goods are made is currently taking place, and it is intimately related to how the [IoT](https://www.sciencedirect.com/topics/physics-and-astronomy/internet-of-things "Learn more about IoT from ScienceDirect's AI-generated Topic Pages") will develop in the future. Robotics, [3D printing](https://www.sciencedirect.com/topics/computer-science/3d-printing "Learn more about 3D printing from ScienceDirect's AI-generated Topic Pages"), [ML](https://www.sciencedirect.com/topics/computer-science/machine-learning "Learn more about ML from ScienceDirect's AI-generated Topic Pages"), networking, [data analytics](https://www.sciencedirect.com/topics/computer-science/data-analytics "Learn more about data analytics from ScienceDirect's AI-generated Topic Pages"), and other technological developments are transforming industrial processes and lowering human labour and judgement. By using digital technologies, manufacturing may decrease human error, shorten the time to market, and speed up the rate at which industrial processes can respond to new information. Industry 4.0 refers to the real-time interdependence of process and analytics and the convergence of operational and information technology[[27]](https://www.sciencedirect.com/science/article/pii/S294973612200001X#b27), [[28]](https://www.sciencedirect.com/science/article/pii/S294973612200001X#b28), [[29]](https://www.sciencedirect.com/science/article/pii/S294973612200001X#b29).

Industry 4.0 transforms how companies create, enhance, and distribute their goods. Manufacturers are integrating cutting-edge technology into their production facilities and operations, including [cloud computing](https://www.sciencedirect.com/topics/computer-science/cloud-computing "Learn more about cloud computing from ScienceDirect's AI-generated Topic Pages") and analytics, the [IoT](https://www.sciencedirect.com/topics/computer-science/internet-of-things "Learn more about IoT from ScienceDirect's AI-generated Topic Pages"), [AI](https://www.sciencedirect.com/topics/computer-science/artificial-intelligence "Learn more about AI from ScienceDirect's AI-generated Topic Pages"), and ML. Industry 4.0, called smart manufacturing, integrates physical production and operations with intelligent digital technologies, such as [big data](https://www.sciencedirect.com/topics/physics-and-astronomy/big-data "Learn more about big data from ScienceDirect's AI-generated Topic Pages") and ML, to build a complete and well-connected corporate environment. The manufacturing process must be redesigned with an architecture that can absorb enormous volumes of real-time data produced by [IoT](https://www.sciencedirect.com/topics/engineering/internet-of-things "Learn more about IoT from ScienceDirect's AI-generated Topic Pages") sensors and other devices and enable millisecond control of the whole environment to accomplish this convergence[[30]](https://www.sciencedirect.com/science/article/pii/S294973612200001X#b30), [[31]](https://www.sciencedirect.com/science/article/pii/S294973612200001X#b31), [[32]](https://www.sciencedirect.com/science/article/pii/S294973612200001X#b32), [[33]](https://www.sciencedirect.com/science/article/pii/S294973612200001X#b33).

4. Need of CPS in Industry 4.0
------------------------------

CPS must provide the integration procedures, networking, and tools required. CPS allows us to envision, construct, develop, refine, and perpetuate smart systems in domains that lead to the improvement of companies, communities, and individuals. CPS has the potential to influence technology in a wide range of Industry 4.0. In order to upgrade the various out-of-date systems of Industry 4.0 infrastructure, CPS engineers must become experts in technology[[34]](https://www.sciencedirect.com/science/article/pii/S294973612200001X#b34), [[35]](https://www.sciencedirect.com/science/article/pii/S294973612200001X#b35), [[36]](https://www.sciencedirect.com/science/article/pii/S294973612200001X#b36). Critical infrastructure may be more often when cutting-edge technologies are introduced to physical frameworks. The use of CPS to advance [sustainability](https://www.sciencedirect.com/topics/earth-and-planetary-sciences/environmental-impact-assessment "Learn more about sustainability from ScienceDirect's AI-generated Topic Pages") is growing. In addition to enhancing [agricultural techniques](https://www.sciencedirect.com/topics/agricultural-and-biological-sciences/agricultural-techniques "Learn more about agricultural techniques from ScienceDirect's AI-generated Topic Pages") and enabling scientists to minimise undersea oil leaks, CPS assist firefighters in detecting and preventing fires. CPS advancements will make systems significantly more capable, adaptable, scalable, resilient, safe, secure, and usable than the current generation of basic embedded systems[[37]](https://www.sciencedirect.com/science/article/pii/S294973612200001X#b37), [[38]](https://www.sciencedirect.com/science/article/pii/S294973612200001X#b38), [[39]](https://www.sciencedirect.com/science/article/pii/S294973612200001X#b39).

CPS is made up of a variety of distinct components. CPS is a concept that stands out for including IoT, smart cities, operational technology, and IT infrastructures. By protecting access and [data security](https://www.sciencedirect.com/topics/engineering/data-security "Learn more about data security from ScienceDirect's AI-generated Topic Pages") using cutting-edge techniques, we can stop any cyber-attack from damaging our physical assets and disrupting our business operations. CPS innovation is built on contributions from several fields, and it needs a trained workforce to support and sustain its growth[[40]](https://www.sciencedirect.com/science/article/pii/S294973612200001X#b40), [[41]](https://www.sciencedirect.com/science/article/pii/S294973612200001X#b41). It can transform and advance the sector while solving several pressing issues. The function of CPS has altered and developed due to the growth in the number of devices linked to the IoT, resulting in increased productivity, efficiency, and financial gains. This offers capabilities for system analysis of the associated [cybernetic](https://www.sciencedirect.com/topics/computer-science/cybernetics "Learn more about cybernetic from ScienceDirect's AI-generated Topic Pages") and [physical structures](https://www.sciencedirect.com/topics/engineering/physical-structure "Learn more about physical structures from ScienceDirect's AI-generated Topic Pages"), as well as improvements in real-time processing, detection, and [actuation](https://www.sciencedirect.com/topics/engineering/actuation "Learn more about actuation from ScienceDirect's AI-generated Topic Pages") between IoT systems and physical domains[[42]](https://www.sciencedirect.com/science/article/pii/S294973612200001X#b42), [[43]](https://www.sciencedirect.com/science/article/pii/S294973612200001X#b43), [[44]](https://www.sciencedirect.com/science/article/pii/S294973612200001X#b44).

5. Research objectives
----------------------

CPS was made possible by the increasing usage of sensor technologies. Factories are connected and spread, enabling clients to choose from various service providers for different manufacturing phases. Industry 4.0 technologies constantly produce enormous volumes of diverse data. Vulnerability and risk may rise as a result of data management and storage. The data must be secure to organise all this information[[45]](https://www.sciencedirect.com/science/article/pii/S294973612200001X#b45), [[46]](https://www.sciencedirect.com/science/article/pii/S294973612200001X#b46). A strong [data security](https://www.sciencedirect.com/topics/engineering/data-security "Learn more about data security from ScienceDirect's AI-generated Topic Pages") strategy and a practical fix for this issue are required. # Cyber-Physical Systems (CPS)

## View guidelines

## NSF's implementation of the revised 2 CFR

NSF Financial Assistance awards (grants and cooperative agreements) made on or after October 1, 2024, will be subject to the applicable set of award conditions, dated October 1, 2024, available on the [NSF website](https://www.nsf.gov/awards/managing/award_conditions.jsp?org=NSF). These terms and conditions are consistent with the revised guidance specified in the [OMB Guidance for Federal Financial Assistance](https://www.coffa.gov/uniform-guidance-coffa/overview/) published in the Federal Register on April 22, 2024.

## Important information for proposers

All proposals must be submitted in accordance with the requirements specified in this funding opportunity and in the NSF [Proposal & Award Policies & Procedures Guide (PAPPG)](/policies/pappg "NSF Proposal & Award Policies & Procedures Guide (PAPPG)") that is in effect for the relevant due date to which the proposal is being submitted. It is the responsibility of the proposer to ensure that the proposal meets these requirements. Submitting a proposal prior to a specified deadline does not negate this requirement.

## Updates to NSF Research Security Policies

On July 10, 2025, NSF issued an [**Important Notice**](/notices/important/important-notice-no-149-updates-nsf-research-security/in149 "Important Notice No. 149: Updates to NSF Research Security Policies") providing updates to the agency's research security policies, including a research security training requirement, Malign Foreign Talent Recruitment Program annual certification requirement, prohibition on Confucius institutes and an updated FFDR reporting and submission timeline.

## Synopsis

The Cyber-Physical Systems program supports convergent research activities to accelerate fundamental and applied advances in cyber-physical systems (CPS) and shape the next generation of intelligent, secure, resilient, and scalable engineered systems. 

Cyber-physical systems (CPS) are engineered systems that are built from, and depend on, the seamless integration of computation and physical components. The new, smart CPS drives innovation and competition in a range of application domains including agriculture, aeronautics, civil infrastructure, electric grid, manufacturing, and transportation. Increasingly artificial intelligence (AI) and machine learning (ML) are being explored to help address the rising complexity of CPS systems. At the same time, the properties of cyber-physical systems including real-time constraints, physical dynamics, and mobility offer the potential to advance the science of AI in support of CPS resilience, verification and assurance, e.g., in the areas of safe learning and neuro-symbolic computing. Similarly, the emerging field of quantum information systems provides an opportunity to design novel solutions to scalability and state space challenges of CPS. 

The CPS program calls for foundational research, including new theoretical frameworks, use-inspired research, and interdisciplinary collaboration.  The program seeks advances in core CPS areas that include control, real-time planning, learning and optimization for control, autonomy, security, real-time scheduling, and formal methods for verification and assurance, based on both traditional and emerging approaches. By abstracting from the particulars of specific systems and application domains, the CPS program seeks to reveal cross-cutting, fundamental scientific and engineering principles that will serve as the backbone for designing the next generation of these systems across all application domains.  

The CPS program supports research proposals that fall into one or both of the following tracks (a) Foundational Research in Cyber-Physical Systems (CPS-FR), and (b) Community-Inspired Cyber-Physical Systems Research (CPS-CIR). Successful proposals in both focus areas will clearly articulate the cyber components, the physical components and the integration between the two, and identify how the project will advance the state of the art in cyber-physical systems, AI for cyber-physical systems, and/or the emerging area of quantum information systems for cyber-physical systems. All proposals are encouragedto provide measurable, clearly defined quantitative evaluation metrics and describe how research outcomes will be validated in real-world contexts.  In addition to research proposals, the CPS program would consider proposals for workshops and similar activities that aim to identify and address emerging needs of future CPS and motivate revolutionary new directions of CPS research. 

Foundational Research in Cyber-Physical Systems (CPS-FR) 

The CPS-FR track supports research that advances the core scientific and engineering principles underlying the integration of computation and physical processes. Projects in this track generate broadly applicable theories, algorithms, methods, tools, and testbeds that can enable robust, AI-driven CPS across multiple domains. While proposals should abstract from specific applications to identify cross-cutting solutions, the program also welcomes focused research in a single domain, if it is grounded in CPS fundamentals and has the potential to revolutionize that field. Validation of research contributions through prototyping or testbeds is encouraged. Areas of interest include: neuro-symbolic approaches to counter scalability and tractability challenges, AI-enabled autonomy (e.g., physics-informed learning and real-time decision-making), quantum information systems for CPS, control and dynamics for complex systems, co-design of time-sensitive applications and resource constrained platforms, formal methods for verification and assurance for CPS with AI systems, and novel approaches to CPS cybersecurity. 

Community-Inspired Cyber-Physical Systems Research (CPS-CIR) 

The CPS-CIR track supports interdisciplinary research driven by real-world challenges identified by well-defined communities of practice, such as system developers, operators, end users, and populations directly impacted by CPS technologies. These community representatives serve as critical collaborators who help shape research questions, define requirements, identify gaps, and provide ongoing feedback and evaluation. The CPS-CIR proposals clearly define the target community and the establish active partnerships with its representatives to ensure the work remains grounded in actual needs. The goal is to co-develop innovative, use-inspired CPS solutions that advance fundamental science and engineering while delivering measurable improvements in practice. Areas of interest include but are not limited to transportation, disaster response, infrastructure resilience, agriculture, and electric grid.

enriched_points.expanded_content: Collaboration among industry stakeholders enhances CPS safety, fosters innovation, and drives the development of resilient systems by leveraging shared expertise and resources.
Active partnerships between government entities and CPS developers promote regulatory compliance, enhance public safety, and facilitate funding for research and development initiatives.
Exchanging threat intelligence across sectors enables organizations to proactively address vulnerabilities, adapt to emerging risks, and strengthen overall cybersecurity measures in CPS environments.
